grading.rationale:
All questions are answered correctly and fully according to the rubric. 

Q1: The method checks for even length and returns false if odd (correct). It sums the first half and second half correctly with correct loop bounds, and returns true only if sums are equal. No deductions.

Q2 Part A: The Team class declares all three fields as private, has a constructor initializing all fields, and provides standard getters and setters for all attributes. No deductions.

Q2 Part B: The Hackathon class declares the required properties, constructor initializes the array and currentTeams correctly, addTeam checks for space and increments currentTeams, returns correct boolean, and countComputersNeeded sums numMembers only for teams needing computers using getters. No direct field access violating encapsulation. No deductions.

Q3: The isValidPassword method is static, checks length >= 8, checks for uppercase letter and digit, and returns true only if all conditions met. No inverted return. No deductions.
